task: BOUND-1 -- DEEP-SHELL charge kappa_j for j>=2: prove t_j <= tau_j rigorously via anchor-lattice separation min|M^j v|_inf; give exact per-shell tau_j for j=0,1,2,>=3 on r in [1,10]; test exact max t_j over all centres at L6/L7.
core_lemma: Birth-shell-j set S_j = M^j . interiors(k-j) is a subset of the lattice M^j Z^3 (stitch interiors are integer points; birth-telescoping dilates a level-(k-j) interior by M^j). Hence t_j(q,r) = #{S_j cap Cheb-r box(q)} <= #{ M^j Z^3 cap Cheb-r box } =: P_j(r): a FIXED-LATTICE count, LEVEL-INDEPENDENT, non-circular (never references c_k).

separations_min_Mjv_inf_EXACT.rigor: First row of M^j is [3^j,0,0], so (M^j v)_0 = 3^j v_0; any v_0!=0 gives inf-norm >= 3^j > candidate, forcing v_0=0. That reduces the minimisation to a bounded 2D integer lattice, min attained in a small box (exhaustively verified). Attained at: j1 v=(-1,-1,-1)->3, j2 v=(0,0,-1)->8, j3 v=(0,-1,0)->24.
separations_min_Mjv_inf_EXACT.packing_consequence: A closed Cheb-r box has side 2r. If 2r < min|M^j v|_inf then it holds <=1 point of M^j Z^3, so t_j <= 1. => t_1<=1 for r<1.5 (r=1); t_2<=1 for r<4 (r=1,2,3); t_3<=1 for r<12 (ALL r in [1,10]).

per_shell_tau_r_1_to_10.tau_0: OUT OF DEEP-SHELL SCOPE. birth-shell-0 = interiors(k) subset Z^3; lattice count = (2r+1)^3 is useless. tau_0 is the RESIDUAL single-level refill lemma (measured t_0 <= 3r, slope<=3). Not closed by BOUND-1.
per_shell_tau_r_1_to_10.tau_1: SHALLOW. Rigorous only at r=1 (min|Mv|=3 => t_1<=1). For r>=2 the lattice bound P_1 (dense M Z^3) is far too loose; measured t_1 <= ~0.8 r + 1. Residual (refill of shallow shell).
per_shell_tau_r_1_to_10.tau_2_notes: RIGOROUS, level-uniform (birth-shell-2 subset M^2 Z^3). SHARP for r<=4 (=measured). For r>=5 loose vs measured max 3. Sharp sub-bound: t_2<=1 for r<=3 via min|M^2 v|=8.
per_shell_tau_r_1_to_10.tau_ge3_notes: RIGOROUS, level-uniform, needs NO induction hypothesis. The ENTIRE deep tail j>=3 = M^3 . Walk_{k-3} subset M^3 Z^3; min|M^3 v|_inf = 24 > 2r for all r<12, so any Cheb-r box (side 2r<=20<24) holds <=1 tail point. P_3(r)=1 confirms.

kappa_vs_t_clarification: The task's per-anchor charge kappa_j (interiors donated PER birth-(k-j) anchor) is measured =1 for j>=2, but that is NOT the same as t_j<=1 (t_j sums over the possibly-several shell-j anchors near the ball). Literally 't_j<=1 for all j>=2, all r<=10' is FALSE for j=2 (measured t_2 up to 3 at r>=6). The correct rigorous statements: t_{>=3}<=1 on all r in [1,10]; t_2<=1 only for r<=3; t_2 <= P_2(r) (<=18) on all [1,10].

verification.level_stability_t2_K6_K7_K8: max t_2 = 3 at L7 and L8 (r>=8), matched by L6 tail; stable.

circularity: RESOLVED for deep shells. t_j <= P_j(r) uses ONLY the fixed lattice M^j Z^3 and r; it never references the crowding count c_k, so no induction/feedback (contrast refill Route-1's STAR bound which looped through c_k with divergent coeff 20/9). tau_{>=3}=1 needs no hypothesis at all. Shallow shells tau_0, tau_1 remain the residual (non-deep) single-level refill lemma.
status: RIGOROUS-BOUND for the deep shells (BOUND-1's target): tau_{>=3}=1 exactly and tau_2 <= P_2(r) via finite level-independent lattice-point counts. Shallow tau_0, tau_1 are explicitly out of deep-shell scope (residual refill lemma).
